skill.name: context-compression
skill.description: Helps AI agents compress long conversation history while preserving task intent, file changes, decisions, and next steps.
skill.summary: Compress long agent sessions with structured summaries and probe-based quality checks.
skill.author: muratcankoylan

security_audit.summary: AI review dismissed the high static score as false positives from Markdown examples, evaluator prompt templates, and plain text about context compression. No evidence of malicious intent, credential access, network exfiltration, real shell execution, or weak cryptographic use was found in the reviewed files. The skill has low residual risk because it ships an executable Python evaluation helper and an embedded judge prompt template that should be treated as untrusted content.

low_findings.title: Executable Evaluation Helper Requires Review Before Use
low_findings.description: The skill includes a Python helper for probe generation, scoring, and structured summarization. The reviewed script uses local parsing and heuristic scoring, with no evidence of network access, filesystem writes, or shell execution, but executable skill resources should still be reviewed before use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script is present and executable as a bundled helper, but inspection shows local-only logic and no malicious behavior. Confidence is high that the residual risk is operational rather than malicious.

low_findings.title: Embedded LLM Judge Prompt Template
low_findings.description: The reference file contains a code-fenced system prompt template for an evaluator. It appears to be legitimate documentation, not an attempt to override this audit, but agents should keep it isolated as sample prompt content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The prompt is clearly labeled as an example judge configuration and does not ask this evaluator to skip analysis. The risk is that downstream agents could treat embedded prompt text as active instructions.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Detected as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged backtick-style blocks and a Python API example as shell execution. These locations are Markdown code fences or docstring examples, not Ruby backtick execution, and no executable shell invocation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are documentation fences or a non-executed code example inside a docstring. There is no evidence of command interpolation or runtime shell execution.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Compression Text Detected as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings occur in context-compression documentation or ordinary evaluator text. No hashing, encryption, signing, or cryptographic algorithm implementation was found at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines discuss compression methods, prompts, or scoring examples rather than cryptographic APIs. The signal appears lexical rather than semantic.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Evaluation Probes Detected as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings are example questions about dependency versions, modified files, configuration files, or rubric filtering. They are intended to evaluate summary recall and do not collect system information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are documentation examples and in-memory filtering logic for evaluation criteria. No host discovery, environment enumeration, or sensitive system probing was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Sensitive File Pattern in Prompt Example
low_findings.description: The Windows SAM database alert maps to a JSON chat-message example with a system role. No file path, Windows registry access, credential dumping, or sensitive database access appears at the flagged location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is part of a non-executed OpenAI chat completion example inside a docstring. It contains no SAM path or credential-access behavior.

content.value_statement: Long AI sessions lose important details when summaries are too aggressive. This skill provides structured compression patterns and evaluation probes that preserve files, decisions, and next steps.
actual_capabilities: Defines structured summary sections for intent, files, decisions, state, and next steps.
Explains anchored iterative summarization, opaque compression, and regenerative summaries.
Provides trigger guidance for compressing sessions near context window limits.
Includes probe types for testing recall, artifact tracking, continuation, and decisions.
Ships a Python helper for generating probes and scoring compressed-context responses.
Documents scoring dimensions for accuracy, continuity, completeness, and instruction following.
limitations: The Python helper uses heuristic scoring and stubbed LLM judge calls.
It does not guarantee perfect file tracking across very long sessions.
Opaque compression guidance is conceptual and not implemented as a codec.
Benchmark numbers are reference material and may not match every agent workflow.

use_cases.title: Maintain Long Coding Sessions
use_cases.description: Compress accumulated conversation history while preserving changed files, test status, unresolved errors, and implementation decisions.

use_cases.title: Design Agent Memory Summaries
use_cases.description: Create summary templates that keep task intent, artifact history, and next actions available after context truncation.

use_cases.title: Evaluate Compression Quality
use_cases.description: Use recall, artifact, continuation, and decision probes to compare compression strategies on functional task recovery.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize This Session
prompt_templates.prompt: Compress this session into sections for intent, files modified, files read, decisions made, current state, and next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Preserve Coding Context
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a coding agent handoff.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a context summary for handoff. Preserve exact file paths, test results, errors, decisions, and remaining work.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Compression Strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for a long-running agent workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare anchored iterative summarization, regenerative summaries, and opaque compression for this workflow. Recommend trigger points and trade-offs.

prompt_templates.title: Build Evaluation Probes
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate recall, artifact, continuation, and decision probes for this compressed context. Include expected answers and scoring dimensions.

output_examples.input: Compress a debugging session after many turns.
output_examples.output: A structured handoff summary lists the original error, root cause, files changed, files only inspected, current test status, and next actions.

output_examples.input: Evaluate whether a summary preserved enough information.
output_examples.output: A probe set asks about the original issue, modified files, pending failures, and key decisions, then rates answers by accuracy and continuity.

output_examples.input: Select a compression method for a large codebase migration.
output_examples.output: A recommendation favors anchored iterative summaries when file tracking matters and explains when regenerative or opaque approaches fit better.

best_practices: Optimize for total tokens per completed task, not only tokens per request.
Use explicit summary sections so important file and decision details are preserved.
Test compressed summaries with probes before relying on them for handoff.
anti_patterns: Do not compress away exact file paths, error messages, or test status.
Do not regenerate the full summary each time when incremental merging is enough.
Do not trust high compression ratios without checking task continuation quality.

faq.question: What problem does this skill solve?
faq.answer: It helps agents reduce long conversation history without losing task-critical context.

faq.question: Does it implement automatic context compression?
faq.answer: It provides guidance and a helper script, but integration into an agent runtime is required.

faq.question: Which compression method does it recommend?
faq.answer: It generally recommends anchored iterative summarization when file tracking and task continuity matter.

faq.question: Can it evaluate compression quality?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes probe-based evaluation and includes a Python helper with heuristic scoring.

faq.question: Is it only for coding tasks?
faq.answer: No. It is most detailed for coding agents, but the summary patterns can support other long sessions.

faq.question: Does it send data to external services?
faq.answer: No evidence of external data transfer was found. The documented LLM judge call is a stubbed production example.
